title: Lightly Obscured
excerpt: A lightly obscured area interferes with sight enough to reduce visual clarity without fully blocking what lies inside or beyond it.
markdown:
# Lightly Obscured

A lightly obscured area interferes with sight enough to reduce clarity without fully shutting vision down. Dim light, thin fog, drifting smoke, sparse foliage, and similar conditions commonly create light obscurity.

## Rules

- lightly obscured spaces make creatures, objects, and details harder to pick out through normal sight
- the visual penalty is enough to interfere with notice and detail, but not enough to create the same level of blindness as heavy obscurity
- light obscurity usually matters most for checks, searches, and other moments where fine visual detail determines what a creature can reliably notice
- a lightly obscured area can become heavily obscured if the blocking effect thickens, deepens, or stacks with stronger interference

## Perception Pressure

Light obscurity creates partial uncertainty rather than full visual collapse. A creature can usually still tell that something is present, but details, identity, or subtle motion become easier to miss. That makes [[search_action|Search [Action]]] and other deliberate detection efforts more important when the scene is only partly visible.

## Relationship To Other Vision Rules

[[dim_light|Dim Light]] commonly creates a lightly obscured area, while [[heavily_obscured|Heavily Obscured]] spaces block vision far more aggressively. The broader [[obscured_areas|Obscured Areas]] rule describes how both levels of visual interference affect sight-based resolution.
